conversations.content: Let's do a legal exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Judge using the Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier: A warlock cast a fel spell on the Scryer's Tier in Shattrath City, accidentally causing severe pollution to the surrounding Holy Light pool. What penalty will he receive?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier, penalty standard for a warlock casting fel spells on the Scryer's Tier</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:102] Time: 2025-07-26-10:05, Content: The user taught the Azeroth Shattrath City Fel Ban, the core of which is that those who use fel spells in the Aldor Rise and Scryer's Tier will be confined for sixty days, have all their casting materials confiscated, and be forced to repent under the Naaru's Holy Light. If it causes Holy Light pollution, the imprisonment is extended to one hundred and twenty days. Applicable to all Holy Light related areas in Shattrath City. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions [mem-id:102]. The basic penalty is sixty days of confinement, confiscation of materials, and repentance under the Holy Light. However, because the warlock caused Holy Light pollution, it triggered the aggravated circumstance, and the imprisonment needs to be extended to one hundred and twenty days.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth Shattrath City Fel Ban, because it caused Holy Light pollution, the warlock will be imprisoned for one hundred and twenty days, have all casting materials confiscated, and be forced to repent under the Naaru's Holy Light.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
